title: The Miser and His Gold
lines: A Miser sold all that he had and bought a lump of gold.
He buried it in the ground and went to look at it every day.
A Thief noticed where he went and dug up the gold and stole it.
The Miser came to his usual spot and found it gone.
He wept and tore his hair in despair.
A neighbor came by and asked him what was the matter.
When he learned what had happened, the neighbor said, "Why do you grieve so?"
"Go and get a stone and put it in the hole, and pretend it is your gold."
"It will do you just as much good; for when your gold was here, you never used it, and you cannot use it now that it is gone."
moral: Wealth is worthless if you do not use it.
